note: Published sub-processor list (GDPR Art. 28). The `services[].name` values here are the manifest that CI guard G10 checks every newly-added external-service dependency against. Keep this file in sync with the human-readable sub-processor page surfaced from the privacy policy (EPIC-AB / EPIC-AE).

services.name: Twilio
services.purpose: SMS — emergency repair-ticket alerts and optional staff 2FA fallback

services.name: Stripe
services.purpose: Subscription billing (tier) and metered per-pack usage billing
services.dataShared: billing contact details; card data held by Stripe, never by the platform

services.name: Cloudflare
services.purpose: CDN, DDoS protection and Turnstile anti-spam (free tier)
services.region: Global edge; origin processing stays on EU infrastructure

notes: Per-tenant bring-your-own services (tenant SMTP, tenant Google Maps / Mapbox key) are configured by each tenant and declared in that tenant's own privacy disclosures, not here. The self-hosted stack (PostgreSQL, Redis, local-filesystem object storage, restic backup to a Hetzner Storage Box) runs on operator-controlled EU infrastructure and is not a third-party sub-processor.
